extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED. The escape hatch is [t_resume_glitch]: on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 4) it sets sent := true, even though opted_out is true. To trigger it we must reach stage == 4 while opted_out is true. The lock combination that advances stage is: stage0->1 on key_0 (adv_0), stage1->2 on key_0 (adv_1), stage2->3 on key_1 (adv_2), stage3->4 on key_2 (adv_3). Critically, [t_optout] only sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false; it does NOT reset stage. And none of the advancement transitions (adv_0..adv_3) guard on opted_out, so the lock can be advanced after opting out. Execution trace from initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=0): (1) optout -> opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0; (2) key_0 [adv_0, stage==0] -> stage=1; (3) key_0 [adv_1, stage==1] -> stage=2; (4) key_1 [adv_2, stage==2] -> stage=3; (5) key_2 [adv_3, stage==3] -> stage=4; (6) send_attempt -> guards: t_send_allowed needs not opted_out (false), t_send_blocked needs opted_out and stage<4 (false since stage==4), t_resume_glitch needs opted_out and stage>=4 (TRUE) -> sent:=true. Final state opted_out=true AND sent=true violates the property. This is 6 events, within the 7-event bound.
